eff: noun. Alternative spelling of ef; the name of the Latin script letter F / f.
ego: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eggon.
eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.
feign: verb. To make a false show or pretence of; to counterfeit or simulate.
fie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fyer.
fief: noun. Land held of a superior, particularly on condition of homage, fealty, and personal service, especially military service.
fife: noun. A small shrill pipe, resembling the piccolo flute, used chiefly to accompany the drum in military music
fifer: noun. One who plays on a fife.
fig: noun. The fruit of the fig tree, pear -shaped and containing many small seeds.
fin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Finnish.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
finer: adjective. comparative form of fine: more fine
finger: noun. A slender jointed extremity of the human hand, (often) exclusive of the thumb, and the corresponding part, irrespective of including
fino: noun. The driest and palest type of traditional sherry.
fir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Firan.
fire: noun. A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat
firn: noun. A type of old snow which has gone through multiple thaw and refreeze cycles and thus is made of numerous small icy grains, though it
for: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fore.
fore: adjective. Former; occurring earlier (in some order); previous.
foreign: adjective. Located outside a country or place, especially one's own.
forge: noun. A furnace or hearth where metals are heated prior to hammering them into shape.
fro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old French.
froe: noun. A cleaving tool for splitting cask staves and shingles from a block of wood.
frog: noun. Any of a class of small tailless amphibians of the order Anura that typically hop.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
genro: noun. A body of elder statesmen of Japan, formerly used as informal advisors to the Emperor.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
giro: noun. A transfer of funds between different account holders, carried out by the bank according to payer's written instructions.
goer: noun. One who, or that which, goes.
gofer: noun. A worker who runs errands; an errand boy.
goffer: verb. To make wavy; to crimp.
goner: noun. Someone (or something) doomed; a hopeless case, especially someone who is bound to die soon.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.
griffe: noun. A claw -like ornament at the base of a column.
griffon: noun. Alternative form of griffin (legendary creature)
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
groin: noun. The crease or depression of the human body at the junction of the trunk and the thigh, together with the surrounding region.
iff: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ifo.
ignore: verb. To deliberately not listen or pay attention to.
inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
irone: noun. Any of several ketones, or a mixture of such, found in orris oil (oil extracted from iris roots), used as odorants in perfumes.
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
reffing: verb. present participle and gerund of ref
reg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (Tanzania).
region: noun. Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
reign: noun. The exercise of sovereign power.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
rif: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tarifit.
rife: adjective. Widespread, common, prevalent, current (mainly of unpleasant or harmful things).
riff: noun. A repeated melody line in a song, usually instrumental but sometimes vocal.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
